items.name: #Woodworking Table
items.description: For carving. Includes shelves to hold a selection of wood.

items.name: *Pine Torch
items.description: Standard torch, not particularly bright, but functional enough unless it gets too wet. Before attempting to craft, run the Carve or Fletch event from a Woodworking Table.

items.name: -Pine Arrow
items.description: The most basic arrow. Before attempting to craft, run the Carve or Fletch event from a Woodworking Table.

items.name: /Pine Stick
items.description: The most basic weapon, slightly better than fighting unarmed for those who can wield it. You'll generally aim to craft something better, but this may be the result of a minor crafting failure.

items.name: }Pine Bow
items.description: The most basic bow. Before attempting to craft, run the Carve or Fletch event from a Woodworking Table.

items.name: }Improved Pine Bow
items.description: Crafted if major success, or perhaps found.

items.name: _Pine Wood
items.description: Can be carved into weapons or torches or burned for fuel.

items.name: Barometer
items.description: Keep track of what floor the party is on or going to, relative to the entry floor where the danger is least. Useful for generating new rooms.

locations.name: Entry Cavern
locations.description: Torchlit and lined with crafting stations. A large central pillar supports the towering ceiling.

locations.name: D1R1
locations.description: The first room down from the entry floor. Again, there is a skittering in the shadows. Probably a rat like last time, although the squeaking noises feel thinner, grating.

locations.name: Entry Floor Room 2
locations.description: North of Entry Cavern. There is a skittering noise in the shadows.

locations.name: U1R1
locations.description: The first room up from the entry floor. There is a narrow passage, with a suspicious-looking moss all but blocking the middle, and spores are being shot at you from the far end.

locations.name: D1R2
locations.description: Second room on the first floor down from entry floor. From here on, your group will have to decide what items, enemies and rooms are found.

locations.name: Entry Floor Room 4
locations.description: Staircase down, leading to otherworldly enemies that get stronger in the depths: undead, dragons, and demons.

locations.name: U1R2
locations.description: Second room on the first floor up from entry floor. From here on, your group will have to decide what items, enemies and rooms are found.

locations.name: Entry Floor Room 3
locations.description: Staircase up, leading to earthbound enemies that get stronger nearer the surface: animals, plants, humanoid races, constructs.

characters.name: Elf Template
characters.description:
Elves are intelligent and light on their feet, known especially for their Mages.

Duplicate to create either a player char or NPC.

characters.name: Dwarf Template
characters.description:
Dwarves are strong and tough, natural Melee Fighters, but clumsy and poor at magic.

Duplicate to create either a player char or NPC.

characters.name: Halfling Template
characters.description:
Halflings are highly nimble, master Thieves and good with ranged weapons.

Duplicate to create either a player char or NPC.

characters.name: spore colony
characters.description: Connected fungi that launch spores over long distances. Must wait a turn after attempting to attack.

characters.name: moss
characters.description: Slightly sentient green covering the stone floor. Cannot move but can attack slowly in melee. Some advanced species of moss may be able to teleport short distances, but this one can't.

characters.name: Animal Template
characters.description:
Animals rely on natural skills and do not use equipment.

Duplicate to create enemies.

characters.name: rat
characters.description: Somehow they're always bigger when they're coming out of the shadows in an unexplored room.

characters.name: rat skeleton
characters.description: Well, that's not something you'd find in sunlit lands. An underworld force binds and animates its bones.

characters.name: @Thornwick
characters.description: Elf Archer, an example player character. A patch over his left eye has left him slower to react (-1 Dex), but his battle scars have made him resilient (+2 Max HP).

attribs.name: HP
attribs.description: Hit Points — current health of a character.

attribs.name: Max MP
attribs.description: How full magic reserves can be for a character. Also determines MP regen rate.

attribs.name: Poisoned
attribs.description: Turns remaining for the negative status to last.

attribs.name: Str
attribs.description:
Strength increases melee damage dealt.

Choosing the Melee class grants 2 Str, 1 Dex, and 2 Max HP.

attribs.name: Int
attribs.description:
Intelligence increases magic success, reserves (MP), and damage.

Choosing the Mage class grants 2 Int, 1 Dex, and 1 Max MP.

Choosing the Priest class grants 1 Int, 1 Max HP, and 2 Max MP.

attribs.name: Max HP
attribs.description:
Amount when fully healthy. Also determines HP regen rate.

To calculate, add the base species and class stat and multiply the result by XP Level.

attribs.name: Density
attribs.description: Harder is better quality but more difficult to craft.

attribs.name: MP
attribs.description: Magic Points — reserves of magical energy used for casting.

attribs.name: Stamina
attribs.description: Reserves of physical energy used for big actions.

attribs.name: Dex
attribs.description:
Dexterity increases accuracy, evasion, and ranged damage dealt.

Choosing the Thief class grants 3 Dex.

Choosing the Archer class grants 2 Dex and 1 Str.

attribs.name: Evasion
attribs.description: Equipped armour can increase chance to dodge. Combines with character's Dex.

attribs.name: Accuracy
attribs.description: Equipped weapons can increase chance to hit. Combines with character's Dex.

attribs.name: Brightness
attribs.description: More light makes it easier to perceive enemies and details, and may affect light-sensitive creatures. Complete absence of light may even mean running into walls.

attribs.name: Damage
attribs.description: Equipped weapons can increase damage. Combines with character's Dex or Str.

attribs.name: XP
attribs.description:
Experience Points are gained through combat. Defeating an enemy yields 10 × the enemy's XP level to each party member.

When high enough, increase XP Level.

attribs.name: Phys Dmg Reduction
attribs.description: Equipped or natural armour can reduce damage.

attribs.name: Burning
attribs.description: Turns remaining for the negative status to last. Fire does extra damage to undead.

attribs.name: Depth
attribs.description: Dungeon level. Could be specified in feet or meters, but it's simpler to use the number of floors. Determines chance of encountering higher level monsters and better items.

events.name: Perception
events.description:
Chance to detect items or stealthy characters, or avoid detection.

• Int and Dex increase detection.
• Dex increases stealth.

events.name: Initiative
events.description:
Determine who goes first in combat.

However, this roll may not be needed if there is another way to determine who goes first. For example, if one party is stealthed and not detected, then they go first.

events.name: Populate Room — Number of Enemies
events.description:
Higher XP level means that more enemies may spawn. Also roll for:
• [Which Enemy](/play/event/7)
• [Grid Coordinates](/play/event/9)

events.name: Grid Coordinates
events.description: Find out where something is placed.

events.name: Carve or Fletch
events.description:
Shape bow and attach string. Success requires dexterity. Fletching arrows is similar.

• Major success produces a bow with improved stats.
• Minor success results in a standard bow.
• Minor failure produces a bow with poor stats, or fewer arrows.
• Major failure consumes resources and may cause injury.

events.name: Shoot — Success
events.description:
Determines whether a ranged attack hits. Dexterity increases accuracy, while evasion decreases it.

If successful, also roll [Shoot — Dmg](/play/event/6) to determine how much damage is done and apply to a target.

Major failures cause destruction of ammo and may give the enemy a chance to counterattack.

events.name: Melee — Success
events.description:
Determines whether a melee attack hits. Dexterity increases accuracy, while evasion decreases it.

If successful, also roll [Melee — Dmg](/play/event/13) to determine how much damage is done and apply to a target.

events.name: Shoot — Dmg
events.description:
Dex increases both die min and max, while Str and damage from gear increase die max only.

First roll [Shoot — Success](/play/event/5) to determine if the attack hits.

events.name: Melee — Dmg
events.description:
Str increases both die min and max, while damage from gear increases die max only.

First roll [Melee — Success](/play/event/14) to determine if the attack hits.

events.name: Populate Room — Which Enemy
events.selection_strings:
(none)
rat
skeleton rat
moss
spore colony

events.name: Populate Room — Which Item
events.selection_strings:
(None)
Pine Wood
Bowstring
Improved Pine Bow
Pine Bow
Pine Stick
Pine Arrow
Pine Arrow
Stone
Stone

events.name: Populate Room — Number of Items
events.description:
After leaving the entry floor, divide die min by depth, giving a better chance for items.

If outcome is more than 0, then also roll for:
• [Which Item](/play/event/12)
• [Grid Coordinates](/play/event/9)

events.name: Drops
events.description:
Number of items dropped by an enemy on death. Add their XP level to max die.

If result is greater than 0, then roll for [which item](/play/event/12).

events.name: @Magic Missile
events.description: Magical attack that does physical damage similar to shooting an arrow. Cannot miss but may do 0 damage. Consumes MP.

overall settings.title: Dungeon Crawl
overall settings.description: Your party was responding to a distress signal when suddenly, the ground below collapsed, and all fell down a nearly vertical shaft. No one seems to be injured (yet). Doubtless there are enemies in the surrounding caverns — good source of XP, an optimist would say. Modeled after traditional roguelikes and tabletop RPGs. Start by creating your player characters based on templates for species and class. Then craft rudimentary supplies before embarking on an escape to the surface or delving deeper.
